macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Raw response
items.summary:
I appreciate your request, but I need to be transparent with you: I don't have access to real-time web search capabilities or current market data. My knowledge was last updated in April 2024, which is before the date you've specified (April 1, 2026).

To get accurate, current macroeconomic and stock market news from the past 24 hours, I recommend:

1. **Financial News Sites**: Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC, MarketWatch, Financial Times
2. **Market Data Platforms**: Yahoo Finance, Google Finance, Trad

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Ends March Up 1.62%, Escapes Six Consecutive Declines
items.summary: Bitcoin rebounded above $68,000 following US-Iran reconciliation signals, closing March with a 1.62% gain and avoiding six straight monthly drops. Historically, April has seen Bitcoin rise in 8 of 13 months with an average return of 13.06%.[2]

items.title: US Senators Introduce Mined in America Act to Nationalize Bitcoin Mining
items.summary: Senators Bill Cassidy and Cynthia Lummis proposed the Mined in America Act on March 30 to certify US-based Bitcoin mining, phasing out rigs from foreign adversaries like China, which supplies 97% of machines, to secure supply chains.[3]

items.title: Bitcoin Price Volatile, Rises to $68,400 Ahead of Trump Iran Announcement
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped to $65,000 on Monday before surging to $68,400 amid geopolitical developments, including anticipation of a key Trump announcement on Iran war.[4]

items.title: Q1 2026 Crypto Market Crash: Bitcoin Down 46% from ATH
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 46% from its all-time high and 30% since January, with Ethereum down nearly 50%; Fear and Greed index in extreme fear for 46 days straight.[1]

items.title: Hong Kong Delays First Compliant Stablecoin Licenses
items.summary: The launch of Hong Kong's first batch of compliant stablecoin licenses has been postponed, with the Monetary Authority working to advance the process.[2]

items.title: TD Cowen: Low Odds for US Crypto Market Structure Bill Passage
items.summary: TD Cowen assesses only a one-in-three chance of a cryptocurrency market structure bill passing in 2026.[2]

items.title: Chainlink Sees 62% QoQ Growth Amid Major Adoptions
items.summary: Chainlink (LINK) achieved 62% quarter-over-quarter growth with $18B in monthly cross-chain transactions; JPMorgan and UBS using it for settlements, and Bitwise Chainlink ETF listed on NYSE Arca.[1]

items.title: Cardano Launches Midnight Privacy Sidechain with Van Rossem Fork
items.summary: Cardano is launching its Midnight privacy sidechain alongside the Van Rossem hard fork before end of March.[1]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Boris FX Acquires Vegas Pro with AI-Enhanced Features in Vegas Pro 2026
items.summary: Boris FX, a developer of AI and machine learning tools for visual effects, has acquired Vegas Pro, announcing Vegas Pro 2026 with new AI improvements for depth modeling, transcription, voice generation, and offline speech-to-text.

items.title: Oracle Begins Mass Layoffs in India Amid AI Data Center Expansion
items.summary: Oracle is conducting significant layoffs potentially affecting up to 12,000 jobs in India, targeting roles in cloud infrastructure, engineering, data centers, and AI/ML functions as part of its push into AI infrastructure.

items.title: Bluesky Launches AI Feature 'Attie' Sparking User Backlash Over Data Usage
items.summary: Bluesky's new AI launch called Attie has triggered user backlash, raising concerns about AI data use, consent, and trust on the platform.

items.title: Nothing Plans AI-Powered Smart Glasses and Earbuds for Upcoming Launches
items.summary: Nothing is developing smart glasses with cameras, microphones, and speakers offloading AI to smartphones and cloud, set for 2027, along with AI-focused earbuds later this year as part of a multi-device strategy.

items.title: IQVIA Highlights Rapid AI Adoption in Pharma for Clinical Decision-Making
items.summary: A survey shows 54% of healthcare professionals using generative AI for scientific information, with higher adoption among younger HCPs, reshaping pharma engagement and go-to-market models.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Goat Simulator 3 Releases on Nintendo Switch 2 Today
items.summary: Goat Simulator 3 launches on April 1 for Nintendo Switch 2, featuring open-world chaos, minigames, and multiplayer. Players can cause mayhem as a goat in stylish outfits.[1][2]

items.title: April 2026 Video Game Release Lineup Announced
items.summary: Major April releases include Darwin's Paradox! and Morkull Ascend to the Gods on April 2, Starfield on April 7, Hades II on April 14, and more across platforms like Switch 2 and PS5. Highlights feature RPGs, simulations, and Xbox exclusives coming to PlayStation.[1][2][3]

items.title: Pokémon Champions Set for April 8 on Nintendo Switch
items.summary: Pokémon Champions, a battle-focused game with familiar mechanics like types, Abilities, and moves, launches April 8 on Nintendo Switch for strategy experimentation.[2]

items.title: Mimikyu Rumored to Debut in Pokémon Go on April 1
items.summary: Reliable source Serebii confirms Mimikyu's arrival in Pokémon Go on April 1 with form-changing mechanics, though fans remain skeptical due to April Fool's timing.[4]

items.title: Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition Coming April 16
items.summary: Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition for Nintendo Switch 2 releases April 16 with updated story, enhancements like improved resolution and frame rates.[2]

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Teams Up with IKEA in Japan
items.summary: Pokémon Pokopia crossover with IKEA starts April 1 in Japan, featuring themed store areas and stamp rallies.[5]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Iran Threatens US Tech Companies with Attacks Starting April 1
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards have named 18 US technology and industrial companies, including Microsoft, Apple, Google, Intel, IBM, Tesla, and Boeing, as targets for potential attacks on their regional operations, citing retaliation for attacks on Iranian territory. The IRGC warned attacks could begin at 8 PM Tehran time on April 1st.

items.title: US Federal Government Partial Shutdown Impacts Department of Homeland Security
items.summary: An ongoing partial federal government shutdown continues to affect employees across the Department of Homeland Security and other agencies.
